title: Library Adapter Preparation for Dual-Index Double Stranded DNA  Illumina Sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cqrkd","text":"Adapter preparation protocol for doubble-stranded ancient DNA libraries for Illumina Next-Generation-Sequencing, based on Meyer and Kircher et al. (2010) Cold Spring Harb. Protoc. (doi: 10.1101/pdb.prot5448). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":140,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":154,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":186,"length":20}],"data":[]},{"key":"854g0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpbt7","text":"The protocol describes the production of an adapter mix for 2000 libraries.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ehphb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"chj2j","text":"This protocol is used in conjunction with Meyer and Kircher-based ancient DNA libraries and is described accordingly (see library construction like: Non-UDG treated double-stranded ancient DNA library preparation for Illumina sequencing).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":66,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"FE48240087C111EA9DBBF54AC769B0D9","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.1101/pdb.prot5448"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4jelt","text":"Working in an Ancient DNA Laboratory","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"avovt","text":"- All steps of the protocol should take place in a clean room facility specifically designed for ancient DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6v16n","text":"- The researcher performing lab work should wear correspondingly suitable lab-wear, such as:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9eism","text":"   - full-body suit with hood (e.g., Tyvek)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6jrch","text":"   - hairnet","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1aksb","text":"   - face mask","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fe491","text":"   - two pairs of clean gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cncru","text":"   - clean shoes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bv8e7","text":"   - protective glasses","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"68a4","text":"- Sample processing should be carried out in separated work benches with integrated UV irradiation (e.g. Dead Air PCR work bench)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e1qqu","text":"- Surfaces and equipment should be regularly decontaminated with e.g. bleach solution or Thermofisher's DNA AWAY (or similar) and irradiated with UV.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1e2ff","text":"- All home-made buffers should be prepared in a seperate decidated PCR-free ultra-clean room and UV-irradiated for 30 min.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ar1ah","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6gumm","text":"Please see the following for more detailed guidance: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7l0ij","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dq051","text":"Llamas, B. et al., 2017. STAR: Science \u0026 Technology of Archaeological Research, 3(1), pp.1–14. Available at: https://doi.org/10.1080/20548923.2016.1258824.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":158,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":242,"length":45}],"data":[]},{"key":"etq59","text":"All steps of the protocol should take place in a clean room facility specifically designed for ancient DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dep26","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ug93","text":"Protocol Specific Guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2djn3","text":"This protocol requires the use of a two rooms - a buffer preparation room and a library preparation room.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"2D6372F687C211EAA1D20242AC110006","url":"https://doi.org/10.1080/20548923.2016.1258824"}}}}

steps: Prepare P5 adapter ( 100 µL, per reaction ) Use one tube of a 0.2 ml PCR strip to set up the P5 adapter mix. %s %s Gently pipette up anddown to mix. Split the 100 µL reaction into two tubes of the 0.2ml PCR strip with 50 µL, each .
Prepare P7 adapter ( 100 µL, per reaction ) Use one tube of a 0.2 ml PCR strip to set up the P7 adapter mix. %s %s Split the 100 µL reaction into two tubes of the 0.2ml PCR strip with 50 µL, each .
Prepare oligo hybridization buffer (10× concentration, 1 mL, for 50 reactions ) %s %s The oligo hybridization buffer can be stored at room temperature for up to one year.

Combine one P5 adapter reaction with one P7 adapter reaction in one tube, mix thoroughly by flicking the tubes with a finger, spin down briefly. Repeat with the second P5 and P7 adpater reaction. In the end you should have two 0.2 ml tubes with a total of 100 µL volume, each containing 50 µL of the P5 adapter and 50 µL of the P7 adapter.
Incubate both reactions in a thermocyler with a heated lid at 95 °C for 00:00:10 , followed by a cool down ramp from 95 °C to 12 °C at a rate of 0.1 °C, per sec .

Add 1800 µL of UV HPLC-water to dilute the double-stranded library adapter mix to 10 micromolar (µM) and aliquot the dilution in 10 × 1.5 ml LoBind tubes, each containing 200 µL . Briefly vortex and spin down before freezing.
Combine both reactions into a 2 ml tube to obtain a ready-to-use double-stranded library adapter mix with 100 micromolar (µM), adapter (each) .
Irradiate the buffer with UV for 00:30:00 without the lid or with an open lid.

Store the adapter mix at -20 °C Each aliquot contains sufficient double-stranded library adapter mix for 200 reactions (overall 2000 libraries). The adapter mix aliquot can be thawed and re-frozen with no detriment to the reagent quality.
creator: Franziska Aron
